title: Mow Rotom Battle Guide
summary: An [Electric]/[Grass] special attacker with [levitate] — [leaf-storm] is the hardest-hitting nuke in the Rotom family, paired with reliable [volt-switch].
bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
Mow Rotom is the hardest single-hit damage dealer in the Rotom family thanks to [leaf-storm]. [Electric]/[Grass] STAB covers [Water], [Ground], and [Rock] targets that are difficult to answer with other Rotom forms, and [levitate] preserves the [Ground] immunity that defines the line.

## Strengths
[leaf-storm] at 130 BP plus [Grass] STAB and 105 SpA is a genuine nuke. Pairing it with [volt-switch] gives a clean way to reset the Special Attack drop by pivoting out. [Electric]/[Grass] typing handles [Water]/[Ground]/[Rock] attackers smoothly while [levitate] keeps [Ground] immunity.

## Weaknesses & Answers
Five weaknesses — [Fire], [Ice], [Bug], [Poison], [Flying] — make switch-ins riskier than the defensive Rotom forms. [leaf-storm] drops SpA by 2, so one commitment means you need to pivot or Tera to stay relevant.

## Recommended Moveset
[leaf-storm] is the nuke. [thunderbolt] is the reliable STAB. [volt-switch] pivots while resetting the SpA drop. [protect] is standard.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[choice-specs] is the offensive default. [life-orb] offers move flexibility. [levitate] is locked. Tera [Grass] stacks [leaf-storm], Tera [Electric] stacks [thunderbolt].

## Teammates & Synergy
Cover the multiple weaknesses with [Fire], [Rock], and [Electric] partners. [tailwind] support extends the offensive window. Pair with [Water] attackers that benefit from [Grass] coverage on the opposite side.

## Play Patterns
Open with [leaf-storm] on a [Water], [Ground], or [Rock] target for the biggest damage. [volt-switch] out immediately to reset the SpA drop. Use [thunderbolt] for sustained pressure when [leaf-storm] is on cooldown.
